data.title: Bitcoin Correction Deepens – Traders Cautious As Downside Pressure Builds Further
data.description:
Bitcoin price corrected gains and traded below the $124,000 level. BTC is now struggling and might continue to move down below $120,000.

  • Bitcoin started a downside correction below the $123,200 level.
  • The price is trading below $123,000 and the 100 hourly Simple moving average.
  • There is a bearish trend line forming with resistance at $122,750 on the hourly chart of the BTC/USD pair (data feed from Kraken).
  • The pair might continue to move down if it trades below the $120,000 zone.

Bitcoin Price Dips Further

Bitcoin price failed to stay above the $125,000 zone and started a fresh decline. BTC dipped below the $124,000 support to enter a short-term bearish zone.

The bears even pushed the price below $121,200. A low was formed at $119,810 and the price recently recovered some losses. There was a move toward the 50% Fib retracement level of the recent decline from the $123,750 swing high to the $119,810 low.

However, the bears are still active near $121,750. Bitcoin is now trading below $121,500 and the 100 hourly Simple moving average. Besides, there is a bearish trend line forming with resistance at $122,750 on the hourly chart of the BTC/USD pair.

Immediate resistance on the upside is near the $121,750 level. The first key resistance is near the $122,250 level and the 61.8% Fib retracement level of the recent decline from the $123,750 swing high to the $119,810 low.

The next resistance could be $122,750 and the trend line. A close above the $122,750 resistance might send the price further higher. In the stated case, the price could rise and test the $123,500 resistance. Any more gains might send the price toward the $124,000 level. The next barrier for the bulls could be $125,500.

More Losses In BTC?

If Bitcoin fails to rise above the $122,750 resistance zone, it could start a fresh decline. Immediate support is near the $120,750 level. The first major support is near the $120,000 level.

The next support is now near the $118,500 zone. Any more losses might send the price toward the $116,500 support in the near term. The main support sits at $115,500, below which BTC might struggle to recover in the short term.

Technical indicators:

Hourly MACD – The MACD is now gaining pace in the bearish zone.

Hourly RSI (Relative Strength Index) – The RSI for BTC/USD is now below the 50 level.

Major Support Levels – $120,750, followed by $120,000.

Major Resistance Levels – $122,750 and $123,500.

data.title: Seatrium Shares Extend Losses; Last 6.6% Lower at S$2.28
Monero releases ‘Flourine Fermi’ update to fight spy nodes
data.description:
Privacy blockchain Monero has rolled out a new client update to provide users with greater security against “spy nodes” on the network.

The “Fluorine Fermi” software update was announced via X on Thursday, with the team stating it is “a highly recommended release.”

The term “spy nodes” is part of the vernacular used in the Monero community. It refers to malicious nodes, groups of nodes, or botnets that have the potential to calculate and match IP addresses to transactions conducted on the network. 

The changes focus on providing greater data protection and node matching protocols, such as an improved peer selection algorithm that works to avoid connecting to multiple nodes in the same range of IP addresses and an increase in the limit of subaddresses (unique one-time addresses) that can be created at once, along with some general reliability and stability fixes. 

Monero privacy threats 

The Fluorine Fermi provides another tool for users to protect their privacy. 

Given that spy nodes threaten the privacy of users in contrast to the main aim of the project, the community has worked hard to find workarounds, establish safety practices, promote self-node operation and utilize software to provide a shield against malicious nodes on Monero. 

One such idea, proposed by the Monero Research Lab in late 2024, is to enable node operators to create a ban list of all IP addresses suspected of being spy nodes, thereby avoiding connections to them.

However, this is not seen as a foolproof or fully sustainable tactic, as malicious node operators could just go on and set up new spy IP addresses if they need to. 

Another tool the community promotes is the use of Dandelion++, software designed to prevent malicious actors from linking IP addresses to transactions.

Monero privacy issues were, in part, brought into the spotlight via a leaked Chainalysis video that appeared in September 2024. 

Cointelegraph was supplied with the video by an anonymous source, which claimed that Chainalysis was able to track transactions back to 2021 via its own “malicious” Monero nodes.

Headline: ‘Help! My robot vac is stealing my Bitcoin’: When smart devices attack

data.title: Genting Malaysia's 2H Earnings Could Decline — Market Talk
data.description: Genting Malaysia's 2H earnings could decline amid a high base in 2Q and larger losses from Empire post-consolidation, Affin Hwang IB analyst Andrew Lim says in a note. He tips 2H earnings between MYR200 million and MYR250 million, compared with 1H's MYR489 million. He expects 2025-2026 earnings to be flat on year as core gaming improvements are likely to be offset by higher losses from its Empire Resorts unit post-consolidation. Affin Hwang raises target price to MYR2.15 from MYR1.95 as it updates the valuation multiple, while maintaining a hold rating on the stock. Shares are 0.7% lower at MYR2.86. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Alkane Resources 1Q Production Lower Than Expected — Market Talk
data.description: Alkane's 1Q result is softer than Moelis Australia expected, with all three of the company's gold mines reporting production marginally below the bank's forecasts, say analysts Paul Hissey and Nic McRostie. "There was no individual asset driver, with the variance broadly distributed across the portfolio," resulting in a 3,600-ounce shortfall overall, the analysts say. "We're unconcerned, however, given the nuance of the timing of deal completion and therefore the commencement of aggregated production," they say, referring to Alkane's merger with Mandalay Resources in August. Cash is also lower than forecast, although that appears in part due to higher-than-expected deal costs, they say. Moelis Australia has a buy rating and A$1.50 target on Alkane, which is down 5.2% at A$1.10. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

data.title: Australia's Guzman y Gomez slips, Morningstar unimpressed with share buyback plan
data.description:
** Mexican-themed fast-food chain Guzman y Gomez slips as much as 4.6% to A$25.180, posting its biggest intraday pct loss since August 28

** Morningstar notes GYG's A$100 mln ($65.49 mln) share buyback with share price trading over A$26

** Investment research firm highlights how GYG issued shares at A$22 each 15 months ago; says selling low and buying high does not create shareholder value

** Morningstar expressed caution about expensive shares, which are trading at 100 times next year's earnings

** Flags no impact on fair value estimate from buyback, maintains PT at A$16 per share

** GYG stock posts record intraday pct jump on Thursday following share buyback plan and results, before closing 2.3% lower at A$26.39

** Stock last down 1.8%, taking YTD losses to 36.3%

($1 = 1.5270 Australian dollars)

data.title: Top Glove Faces Heightened Competition From Expanding China Capacity — Market Talk
data.description: Top Glove may continue to face stiff competition as Chinese glove makers expand into Southeast Asia and adopt aggressive pricing strategies to regain U.S. market share, Maybank IB analyst Wong Wei Sum says in a note. Competition is expected to intensify with new Chinese capacity coming online from November, but should begin to stabilize by FY 2027 as the additional supply is absorbed, she says. Management targets 20%-30% sales volume growth in FY 2026 and expects margins to improve further as plant utilization reaches 80%-90%. Maybank maintains a sell rating on Top Glove and keeps its target price at MYR0.61. Shares are 3.7% higher at MYR0.70.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: India stock benchmarks set to open higher on strong TCS earnings, renewed FPI inflows
data.description:
India's equity benchmarks are set to open marginally higher on Friday, with focus on information technology stocks after Tata Consultancy Services, the country's No. 1 software services firm, posted better-than-expected second-quarter revenue.

Gift Nifty futures (GIFc1) were trading at 25,245 points as of 7:49 a.m. IST, indicating that the benchmark Nifty 50 will open above Thursday's close of 25,181.8.

Nifty 50 has risen in five of the past six sessions, gaining 2.3%, led by about 6% rally in IT stocks .

The sector will remain in focus after TCS beat September-quarter revenue estimates, driven by strength in its banking, financial services and insurance (BFSI) segment.

The company said it expects stronger growth in the second half of fiscal 2026.

However, profits missed estimates, weighed down by a surge in severance costs after the company announced plans to cut 2% of its workforce this fiscal year.

While TCS's results were largely in line, the real catalyst for sentiment was its announcement of a new entity to build world-class artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure, analysts said.

Meanwhile, market sentiment was also boosted by the return of foreign portfolio investors (FPIs) to net buying over the past three sessions.

On Thursday, foreign institutional investors (FIIs) bought shares worth 13.08 billion rupees ($147.3 million) on a net basis, per the National Stock Exchange's provisional data.

Domestic institutional investors have remained net buyers for 32 straight sessions.

While the secondary market showed resilience, the primary market also saw a surge in activity, highlighted by the $1.3 billion maiden share sale by LG Electronics' Indian unit - the most subscribed billion-dollar IPO in nearly two decades.

STOCKS TO WATCH

** Tata Elxsi's (TTEL.NS) profit fell for the fourth straight quarter as the company's transportation business declined amid uncertain markets and geopolitical tensions.

** Mahindra and Mahindra says its total production volume rose 24.4% year-on-year to 99,758 units in September.

** Afcons Infrastructure gets contract worth 5.76 billion rupees.

** NTPC Green Energy signs deal with Gujarat government for solar and wind projects.

($1 = 88.7930 Indian rupees)

data.title: ANZ Forecasts Oil Prices To Trade Around $60-65/BBL Through 1H26
data.description:
ANZ:

  • SUPPLY-DRIVEN SURPLUSES ARE RARELY UNEXPECTED, SO THE MARKET USUALLY PRICES-IN SURPLUS WELL IN ADVANCE, CONSEQUENTLY, EXPECT OIL PRICES TO TRADE AROUND $60–65/BBL, THROUGH FIRST HALF OF 2026

  • FURTHER RECOVERY OF OIL PRICES TO $70/BBL BY END OF 2026 IS POSSIBLE; EITHER DUE TO RISING RECOVERY IN DEMAND OR OPEC STEPPING IN TO SUPPORT MARKET WITH PRODUCTION CUTS

data.title: Indonesia issues tsunami warning after Philippines quake
data.description:
Indonesia has issued a tsunami warning for northern Sulawesi and Papua regions after a strong earthquake hit the southern Philippines on Friday, Indonesia's geophysics agency said.

The agency's modelling suggested there was a risk of tsunami waves as high as 50 cm hitting Indonesia's shorelines, it said in a statement.

data.title: Yen Heads for Worst Week in a Year
data.description:
The Japanese yen traded at an eight-month low around 153 per dollar on Friday and was on track to decline nearly 4% for the week, marking its steepest decline since September last year.

The currency weakened after fiscal dove Sanae Takaichi won Japan’s leadership race to become the next prime minister, fueling expectations of higher fiscal spending and loose monetary policy.

Takaichi said Thursday that the Bank of Japan would set policy independently while aligning with the government’s goals, adding she aimed to avoid excessive yen depreciation, although her comments that did little to support the currency.

Markets now see less than a 50% chance of a BOJ rate hike in December, with expectations shifting toward March next year.

Meanwhile, data showed producer prices in Japan rose more than expected in September, further complicating the rate outlook.

data.title: South Korean shares resume record rally on chipmaker boost
data.description:
Key points:
  • KOSPI rises, foreigners net buyers
  • Korean won weakens against dollar
  • South Korea benchmark bond yield rises

Round-up of South Korean financial markets:

** South Korean shares resumed their record-setting rally on Friday following a local holiday break, underpinned by chipmakers, as optimism around artificial intelligence continued to drive gains.

** The won weakened to a five-month low, while the benchmark bond yield rose.

** The benchmark KOSPI was up 63.95 points, or 1.80%, at 3,613.16, as of 0135 GMT. Earlier in the day, the index climbed as much as nearly 2% to an all-time high.

** Chipmaker Samsung Electronics rose 5.96% to its highest level since January 2021 and peer SK Hynix gained 9.23% to its highest point on record.

** The South Korean chipmakers, which signed a supply deal with OpenAI just before the local holiday period from October 3 to October 9, extended gains for a third consecutive session.

** However, risk appetite remained weak across broader financial markets, with the country's central bank stating that it would closely monitor conditions as global risk factors had slightly increased over the holiday period.

** Shares of steelmaker POSCO Holdings shed 3.85% and Hyundai Steel dropped 1.36%, after the European Union announced higher tariffs on steel imports.

** Among other index heavyweights, battery maker LG Energy Solution slid 10.09%, while Hyundai Motor and sister automaker Kia Corp were down 1.14% and 2.69%, respectively.

** Of the total 929 traded issues, 250 shares advanced, while 649 declined.

** Foreigners were net buyers of shares worth 655.8 billion won ($461.53 million).

** The won was quoted at 1,419.6 per dollar on the onshore settlement platform , 0.89% lower than its previous close at 1,407.0. During the session, it hit its weakest level since mid-May at 1,424.0.

** In money and debt markets, December futures on three-year treasury bonds (KTBc1) lost 0.09 point to 106.70.

** The most liquid three-year Korean treasury bond yield (KR3YT=RR) rose by 3.0 basis points to 2.608%, while the benchmark 10-year yield rose by 2.4 basis points to 2.981%.

($1 = 1,420.9200 won)

data.title: Shanghai Zhida Technology shines in Hong Kong debut
data.description:
** Shares of Shanghai Zhida Technology Development (2650.HK) trade as high as HK$194 in Hong Kong debut

** Stock up 189.9% from offer price of HK$66.92 apiece

** Stock trims gains to HK$191 each in early morning trade, still up 185.4% with 1.2 million shares worth HK$220.9 million ($28.39 million) changing hands

** The Chinese smart home electric vehicle (EV) chargers and accessories producer sold 5.98 million shares in global offering, raising HK$400 million ($51.41 million) for overseas expansion, upgrading production facilities and R&D, among others

** Says subscription level of its Hong Kong public offering portion is 5,440.80 times, while international offering portion is 2.37 times

** The Hang Seng Tech Index drops 2.2% and benchmark Hang Seng Index falls 1.3%

($1 = 7.7806 Hong Kong dollars)

data.title: Genting Malaysia Target Price Raised to MYR2.15 From MYR1.95 by Affin Hwang IB >4715.KU
Yomiuri: MUFG Merger 'Provided Stimulation,' Bank Chair Recalls
data.description:
By Kentaro Otsuka and Shinichi Ikeda

Yomiuri Shimbun Staff Writers

Since its inception in October 2005, Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group Inc. (MUFG) has worked to integrate the two groups it was formed from: Mitsubishi Tokyo Financial Group Inc. and UFJ Holdings Inc. The two banking groups differed in their areas of expertise, regional focus and corporate culture.

"The customer bases and employee cultures of the banks provided mutually beneficial stimulation, yielding many valuable insights," Naoki Hori, chairman of MUFG Bank Ltd., said in a recent interview with The Yomiuri Shimbun.

Hori served as a senior officer in the planning division at UFJ Bank Ltd. before the merger, and afterward, as an executive in charge of human resources, among other posts. The 64-year-old chairman stressed that a "spirit of equality" was key to the merger's success.

The following is excerpted from the interview.

The Yomiuri Shimbun: Before the basic agreement on the merger in 2004, you were involved in negotiations as a senior officer in the planning division of UFJ Bank.

Naoki Hori: I was part of the merger secretariat at UFJ Holdings, which was established in 2001, so I fully understood the methodology and social significance of the merger. I had advised the top management at the time that combining with Mitsubishi Tokyo Financial Group was an excellent move. It would offer superior regional complementarity in terms of primary markets, with the Tokyo metropolitan area (for Mitsubishi Tokyo) and the central and western Japan regions (for UFJ Holdings), and it would better leverage the DNA of the Bank of Tokyo Ltd. (a predecessor to Mitsubishi Tokyo) for global operations.

I remained involved in the merger secretariat until the basic agreement was signed in July 2004. While I wasn't involved in subsequent talks where decisions were made on such issues as the exchange ratio, I argued that merging with Mitsubishi Tokyo was optimal for preserving UFJ's DNA. It offered fields where as many people as possible (from UFJ Holdings) could thrive and promised numerous synergies.

Yomiuri: What did you focus on in terms of personnel development after the merger?

Hori: In the workplace, I consistently stressed that we shouldn't lean toward either UFJ Holdings or Mitsubishi Tokyo, but rather work together to build a new top bank.

I also stressed that, mentally, we must operate with a "spirit of equality," recognizing UFJ Holdings and Mitsubishi Tokyo to be equal. Regarding personnel, I sought to appoint branch managers who were leaders, who could work hard for customers and their colleagues. I tried to place the right people in the right roles and be fair, not being constrained by their former bank affiliations.

The key was ensuring the transfer of expertise: passing on the know-how for large corporate transactions from the Bank of Tokyo-Mitsubishi Ltd. to UFJ Bank personnel and transferring the expertise for mid-size and small business transactions from UFJ Bank to the Bank of Tokyo-Mitsubishi personnel. I was conscious of this when I was in the human resources division and in the corporate and retail unit.

Yomiuri: In which areas are the strengths of the former banks being utilized?

Hori: The Bank of Tokyo-Mitsubishi excelled primarily in large corporate transactions. I found their ability to create high-quality proposals and present them in detail truly impressive. By combining this with UFJ Bank's strengths in mid-size and small business transactions and retail banking, and creating mutual complementarity, I believe we were able to dramatically expand our customer base.

My concern was that everything would become "Tokyo-centric." It was critically important to recognize and share the understanding that central and western Japan, the roots of (UFJ Bank predecessors) Tokai Bank Ltd. and Sanwa Bank Ltd., were also core markets. After the basic agreement, I told the junior colleagues involved in the merger discussions: "We must protect this (understanding) no matter what. Never disappoint our customers in central and western Japan, or the customers who supported the previous banks."

Yomiuri: When you served as the executive in charge of personnel, you cut staff.

Hori: Banking involves a lot of manual work. In digitalizing, we carefully examined how much of the workload could be reduced and concluded this would be equivalent to 9,500 employees. At the time, we anticipated a natural reduction of about 6,000 employees and we went ahead with a plan to reassign the remaining 3,500.

Yomiuri: You also consolidated branches.

Hori: When I became head of the corporate and retail unit, there were just over 500 locations. With the spread of internet banking, customer visits were declining year by year, so we strove to explore further consolidation. Maintaining a branch network incurs significant costs, so we initially started with a 20% reduction, but later consolidated further.

We proceeded with the understanding that keeping branches while customer visits were declining must not result in a deterioration of service quality for our customers. At the same time, we carefully considered whether we could maintain an optimal network for customers in each region.

Yomiuri: What was the greatest challenge in maintaining a "spirit of equality"?

Hori: The crucial point was whether we could continue to maintain close contact with a large number of customers. Many customers, both corporate and individual, continued using their old bankbooks even after the merger. Under no circumstances can we betray the trust and expectations of these loyal customers. Therefore, I believed we had to proceed with a spirit of equality and fairness, and I consistently applied this principle in both operational and personnel matters.

I was confident that our colleagues from the Bank of Tokyo-Mitsubishi would also gain valuable insights, considering the customer base and the character of the UFJ Bank employees. Even before the merger, I knew many people from the Bank of Tokyo-Mitsubishi and was aware of their sober and honest corporate culture. I was sure it would be a good combination.

However, considering the merger environment and the exchange ratio at the time, I felt strongly that we had put our UFJ Bank colleagues through a difficult time. That's precisely why I made every possible effort to integrate the banks.

Yomiuri: In the future, the younger generation will take center stage, and the memory of the old banks will fade. What kind of bank do you want MUFG Bank to become?

Hori: I want it to be an organization that continues to work for our customers and society, no matter the environment or situation, whether domestic or international. Over these past 20 years, I believe we've paved various paths. For the next generation, I hope they will never stop forging better paths, worthy of a top bank, and that they walk steadfastly down those paths.

----

This article is from The Yomiuri Shimbun. Neither Dow Jones Newswires, MarketWatch, Barron's nor The Wall Street Journal were involved in the creation of this content.

YDN-M0000149589-1

data.title: Magnitude 7.4 earthquake strikes Philippines' Mindanao, tsunami warning issued
data.description:
An earthquake of magnitude 7.4 struck Philippines' Mindanao region on Friday, the European-Mediterranean Seismological Centre (EMSC) said.

The quake was at a depth of 58 km (36.04 miles), EMSC said.

The U.S. Tsunami Warning System issued a tsunami threat, saying hazardous tsunami waves are possible for coasts located within 300 km (186 miles) of the earthquake's epicenter.

EMSC had earlier pegged the magnitude at 7.2.

data.title: Key facts: UBS Group maintains dividend strategy; assesses $500M exposure; raises positions in Jiangsu Hengrui
data.description:
• UBS Group plans to keep its cash dividend and share buyback strategy, as CEO Sergio Ermotti confirmed, despite stricter capital rules from Swiss regulators allowing time to build capital.1
  • UBS Group is assessing its $500 million exposure tied to First Brands Group's bankruptcy, which has over $10 billion in liabilities, highlighting risks in the private credit market.2
  • On October 2, 2025, UBS Group AG raised its long position in Jiangsu Hengrui Pharmaceuticals H-shares to 7.48% and its short position to 2.45%.3

data.title: Key facts: S&P upgrades UniCredit to 'A-'; EU reviews Italy's law; takeover bid fails
data.description:
• On October 9, 2025, S&P upgraded UniCredit's Issuer Credit Rating to 'A-', citing better business and financial health. This is S&P's second upgrade for UniCredit in 2025.1
  • The European Commission is reviewing Italy's 'golden power' law, affecting UniCredit by requiring it to end operations in Russia by early 2026, raising EU competition and independence concerns.2
  • UniCredit recently attempted a takeover of Banco BPM, which was ultimately unsuccessful, highlighting ongoing consolidation efforts in the Italian banking sector.3

data.title: Key facts: Novo Nordisk to Acquire Akero for $5.2 Billion; R&D Costs to Rise
data.description:
• Novo Nordisk will acquire Akero Therapeutics for up to $5.2 billion, including $54 per share upfront, a 16.2% premium, to boost its metabolic disease portfolio.123
  • Novo Nordisk's acquisition is expected to raise research and development costs, which may negatively impact operating profit growth by approximately 3 percentage points in 2026.4
  • Akero Therapeutics' deal with Novo Nordisk may incur a $165 million fee if terminated, while Novo Nordisk could face a $185 million fee under specific conditions.5

data.title: Key facts: Chancellor Merz opposes EU's 2035 combustion car ban; Porsche deliveries drop 6%
data.description:
• Chancellor Friedrich Merz met with Volkswagen and other automakers to oppose the EU's 2035 ban on combustion cars, seeking more time to explore alternative energy and boost electric mobility.1
  • Volkswagen's subsidiary Porsche saw a 6% drop in global deliveries in Q3 2025, with a 20% decline in China and 5% in North America due to tough market conditions.2

data.title: Key facts: Airbus delivers 73 aircraft; Turkish Airlines orders 200+; FCAS meeting delayed
data.description:
• Airbus delivered 73 aircraft in September, totaling 507 for the year. The company secured 610 gross orders and aims for 820 deliveries in 2025, needing a 16% rise in Q4.12
  • Turkish Airlines ordered 200+ Airbus planes in 2023 for fleet expansion and may switch a Boeing 737 MAX order to Airbus based on talks with engine supplier CFM International.3
  • The trilateral meeting on the Future Combat Air System (FCAS) involving Airbus has been delayed due to a political crisis in France, impacting the project's timeline.4

data.title: Key facts: Lufthansa considers outsourcing ground services; reports voting rights
data.description:
• Lufthansa is exploring the option of outsourcing its ground services at Frankfurt and Munich airports, as reported by Handelsblatt.1
  • On October 9, 2025, Deutsche Lufthansa AG reported 1,199,282,895 voting rights, complying with the German Securities Trading Act for Europe-wide distribution.2

data.title: Key facts: Ferrari stock drops 15.41%; Citi maintains 'sell' rating; unveils electric sports car
data.description:
• Ferrari's stock price closed at 354.00 euros, reflecting a decline of 64.50 euros, or 15.41%.1
  • Citi keeps a 'sell' rating on Ferrari, noting its 2030 forecast falls short of lower growth estimates, indicating a cautious management style and near-term EPS risks.2
  • Ferrari held its 'Capital Markets Day' on October 9, where the company unveiled its first electric sports car, marking a significant milestone in its product lineup.3

data.title: Key facts: Banco Santander repurchases €603M in shares; UK unit sets £2B for claims
data.description:
• Banco Santander repurchased shares worth 603 million Euros from October 2 to 8, 2025, completing 35.5% of its buyback program. It has bought back 14.6% of its shares since 2021.12
  • Banco Santander's UK unit has set aside over £2 billion for compensation claims related to the motor finance mis-selling scandal, with total sector costs projected at £11 billion.3

data.title: Australia, New Zealand dollars lose to greenback, but gain on yen
data.description:
The Australian and New Zealand dollars looked to be heading for a weekly loss on Friday as their U.S. counterpart benefitted from a broad rally, though the Aussie could still celebrate its best week against the Japanese yen in more than a year.

Even the battered kiwi made solid gains on the yen amid speculation Japan's incoming prime minister would favour a weaker currency and lean against hikes in interest rates.

The Aussie led with a jump of 3.1% for the week so far to 100.38 yen , just off an 11-month top of 100.99.

It had less luck against the U.S. dollar losing 0.5% for the week so far, though it was up a fraction at $0.6568 on Friday. A repeated failure to clear resistance around $0.6624/29 puts the focus on support at $0.6540 and $0.6521.

The kiwi had steadied at $0.5753 , having shed 1.3% on the week and reaching a six-month low of $0.5735. It also struck a three-year trough on the Aussie at A$0.0873 (NZDAUD=R).

The kiwi was undone by a half-point rate cut from the Reserve Bank of New Zealand, which flagged even more easing might be needed to revive a flagging economy.

Markets imply around an 80% probability of a 25-basis-point reduction in November to take rates to 2.25%, and a 36% chance of ending at 2.0%.

Two-year swap rates (NZDSM3NB2Y=) were down 6 basis points for the week at 2.5489, after hitting the lowest since early 2022. Ten-year yields (NZ10YT=RR) dropped 8 basis points to 4.158%, leaving them just 2 basis points above U.S. yields.

The Reserve Bank of Australia has sounded much more cautious about cutting its 3.65% cash rate, with governor Michele Bullock on Friday reiterating concerns about sticky inflation.

Bullock emphasised policy would be driven by economic data, with reports on jobs, inflation and household spending due before the next meeting on November 4.

"There is still enough data to come before the next RBA meeting to justify a November cut, even though currently available information would suggest a hold is more likely," said Luci Ellis, chief economist at Westpac.

"If the RBA does hold the cash rate steady at the November meeting, the chance increases that it cuts in February and ends up at a trough of 2.85% rather than something higher."

data.title: Good luck finding an entry-level crypto job this year, says Dragonfly
data.description:
Securing a job in the crypto industry could be challenging for less-experienced candidates this year, with only 1 in 10 crypto roles at the entry-level, according to a new survey from venture capital firm Dragonfly Capital.

Crypto recruitment executives say the trend may be linked to a surge in failed crypto projects in recent times.

“Crypto was hard to break into; less than 10% of roles were entry-level,” Dragonfly Capital said in a report published on Thursday, citing data collected from 85 crypto companies in late 2024 and Q1 2025, along with approximately 3,400 employee and candidate data points. 

Meanwhile, senior and principal-level positions comprised the largest share of the industry’s roles at 37%. Engineering remained the dominant function across the sample set, accounting for around 67% of total headcount.

Crypto hiring in “cautious” mode this year

Dragonfly stated that, despite a bullish crypto market, rising positive sentiment, and a pro-crypto Trump administration, hiring across the industry has been “cautious,” with net hiring turning negative in the first half of 2025.

“Early swings included strong January job growth, a February tariff shock, and massive reactive job cuts in March,” Dragonfly said.

Entry-level employees bore the brunt of cuts, facing lower salaries and reduced token packages, which were “partly offset by higher equity.”

The results may come as a surprise given crypto’s strong appeal among younger generations. As of December 2024, around one-third, or 34% of global crypto holders were aged between 24 and 35, according to a report by crypto payment company Triple-A.

Kevin Gibson, founder of recruitment firm Proof of Search, said the findings align with his recent observations.

Seasoned crypto staff are back on the market after failed projects

“Despite the overall market positivity and the success of several projects, there have been many instances of projects failing after their token generation events,” Gibson told Cointelegraph.

“This has resulted in many experienced professionals re-entering the job market, giving hiring companies the advantage of selecting from a pool of seasoned candidates,” he said.

Gibson added that, with many expecting the market to remain strong for at least another six months, firms are doubling down on hiring senior talent to accelerate project delivery.

“We have not yet returned to the recruitment landscape we saw in 2021, where entry-level candidates with minimal experience were quickly picked up,” he said.

Matt Thompson, founder of TRB Executive Search, told Cointelegraph that when market conditions are favorable, crypto companies tend to ramp up hiring, but start making layoffs during downturns. 

Thompson said that the volatility itself may deter younger candidates from entering the industry in the long run. “This instability might deter students from pursuing careers in crypto, especially compared to traditional banking, where career longevity is more assured.”

data.title: Dollar Set for Best Week in a Year
data.description:
The dollar index held above 99.3 on Friday and was on track to rise nearly 2% for the week, marking its strongest weekly advance in a year, supported by sharp weakness in the yen and euro.

The yen is poised to drop almost 4% against the dollar this week after fiscal dove Sanae Takaichi won Japan’s leadership race, reinforcing expectations of higher spending and continued monetary easing.

Meanwhile, the euro has fallen about 1.5% versus the dollar amid political turmoil in France, where President Emmanuel Macron continues to search for his sixth prime minister in less than two years.

In the US, the government shutdown extended into its ninth day after the Senate failed to reach a funding deal, delaying key economic data that could guide the Federal Reserve’s rate-cut outlook.

Markets now see a 95% chance of a quarter-point rate cut this month, while odds for a December move have eased to 80% from 90%.

data.title: Fuse Battery Metals Announces Additional Director
data.description:
(TheNewswire)

Coquitlam, BC – TheNewswire - October 9, 2025 – Fuse Battery Metals Inc. (“the Company“ or “Fuse”)(TSXV: FUSE, OTCQB: FUSEFFRA:43W3) is pleased to announce that effectivetoday, it has appointed Andrew Gertler as a Director of the Company. 

Andrew Gertler brings a wealth of insight to his roleas a board director, drawing on a 40-year career in business with aspecialized focus on alternative investments, including real estateand distressed debt.  His expertise was honed through leadershippositions and as an advisor to noteworthy Canadian firms and familybusinesses.  During his time as Senior Vice President at HudsonAdvisors, he handled acquisitions and underwritings for themulti-billion-dollar Lone Star Opportunity Fund, overseeing majortransactions in North American real estate and distressed assets. Spanning beyond real estate.  Mr. Gertler has led successfulengagements in IPOs, mergers, and acquisitions, and has held executiveposts such as CEO of a digital media enterprise and CFO of an onlinenews company.  His management of alternative investments has includedstrategically navigating venture capital, leveraged and managementbuyouts, aligning with institutions like Morgan Stanley and GoldmanSachs.  As a co-founder of Viscount Mining, he raised significantcapital for mineral exploration and was integral in SinomarCapital's alliance with Hunt Mining.  Mr. Gertler’s dynamicleadership at Lester Asset Management and multiple board positionshighlight his strategic prowess and consulting savvy.  With degreesfrom McGill and Western Ontario, his top tier educational foundationamplifies his business acumen, delivering a substantial edge to theFuse team.

In connection with his appointment, the Company isgranting Andrew 100,000 stock options at an exercise price of $0.05per share for a period of two years.  The stockoptions are granted pursuant to the terms of the Company’s stockoption plan, and the requirements of the TSX Venture Exchange.

Mr. Tim Fernback, Fuse President & CEO states“Andrew Gertler is a welcomed addition to the Company’s Board ofDirectors.  His past experience will be a huge benefit to ourshareholders as we continue to expand our team.”

About Fuse Battery Metals Inc. https://fusebatterymetals.com

Fuse Battery Metals Inc. is a Canadian based exploration company that trades under the symbol FUSEon the TSX Venture Exchange. The Company's focus is on exploration for high value metals required for the manufacturingof batteries.

Ontario Cobalt Properties


Fuse owns a 100% interest its Glencore Bucke Property,
situated in Bucke Township, 6 km east- northeast of Cobalt, Ontario,
subject to a back-in provision, production royalty and off-take
agreement. The Glencore Bucke Property consists of 16.2 hectares and sits along the west boundary
of Fuse’s Teledyne Cobalt Project. The Company
also owns a 100% interest, subject to a royalty, in the Teledyne Project located near Cobalt, Ontario. The Teledyne Property adjoins the south and west boundaries of claims that
hosted the Agnico Mine.


Glencore Bucke/Teledyne Property


Situated in Bucke Township, 6 km east-northeast of
Cobalt, Ontario the Glencore Bucke Property adjoins, on its northeast
corner, the former cobalt producing Agaunico Mine. From 1905 through
to 1961, the Agaunico Mine
produced a total of 4,350,000 lbs. of cobalt (“Co”), and 980,000 oz of silver (“Ag”)
(Cunningham-Dunlop, 1979). The
amount of cobalt produced from the Agaunico Mine is greater than that
of any other mine in the Cobalt Mining Camp. Production ceased in 1961 due to depressed Co prices and over-supply (Thomson, 1964). The Glencore property is 100% owned by Fuse Cobalt subject to a
back-in provision, production royalty and off-take agreement.


The associated Teledyne Property, located in Bucke and
Lorrain Townships, consists of 5 patented mining claims totaling 79.1 ha, and 46 unpatented mining claim cells totaling approximately 700 ha. The Property is easily
accessible by highway 567 and a well-maintained secondary
road.


Over CAD$25 million has been spent thus far, (2020
dollars inflation-adjusted) on the Teledyne Property resulting in
valuable infrastructure including a development ramp and a modern
decline going down 500 ft parallel to the main cobalt mineralized vein. The Teledyne Property is subject to a production royalty in favor of New Found Gold and an off-take agreement in favor of Glencore Canada Corp., while the Glencore Bucke Property is subject to a back-in provision, production royalty, and an
off-take agreement in favor of Glencore Canada Corp. Glencore PLC is the world’s largest producer of cobalt. A significant portion
of the cobalt that was produced at the Agaunico Mine was located along structures (Vein #15) that extended southward towards
the northern boundary of the Teledyne Cobalt Property, currently 100% owned by FUSE. Mineralization was generally
located within 125 ft (38.1 m) above the Huronian/Archean unconformity. Stoping widths of up to 50
ft (15.2 m) were not unusual at
the Agaunico Mine (Cunningham-Dunlop, 1979).


On Behalf of the
Board of Directors


“Tim Fernback”


Tim Fernback, President & CEO


Contact Information:


Email: info@fusebatterymetals.com

Phone: 236-521-0207

Neither the TSX Venture Exchange nor its RegulationServices Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSXVenture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracyof this release.

Cautionary Note Regarding Forward Looking Statements:This release contains forward-looking statements that involve risksand uncertainties.  These statements may differ materially fromactual future events or results and are based on current expectationsor beliefs.  For this purpose, statements of historical fact may bedeemed to be forward-looking statements.  In addition,forward-looking statements include statements in which the Companyuses words such as “continue”, “efforts”, “expect”,“believe”, “anticipate”, “confident”, “intend”,“strategy”, “plan”, “will”, “estimate”, “project”,“goal”, “target”, “prospects”, “optimistic” or similarexpressions.  These statements by their nature involve risks anduncertainties, and actual results may differ materially depending on avariety of important factors, including, among others, the Company’sability and continuation of efforts to timely and completely makeavailable adequate current public information, additional or differentregulatory and legal requirements and restrictions that may beimposed, and other factors as may be discussed in the documents filedby the Company on SEDAR (www.sedar.com), including the most recentreports that identify important risk factors that could cause actualresults to differ from those contained in the forward-lookingstatements.  The Company does not undertake any obligation to reviewor confirm analysts’ expectations or estimates or to releasepublicly any revisions to any forward-looking statements to reflectevents or circumstances after the date hereof or to reflect theoccurrence of unanticipated events.  Investors should not place unduereliance on forward-looking statements.

data.title: Indonesian minister says growth could be near 6% or higher in second half of 2026
data.description: Indonesia's economic growth could be around 6% or higher in the second half of 2026, the finance minister said on Friday.
